entry.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
entry.summary:
The discussion on Bitcoin's transition to a post-quantum state emphasizes the necessity of an approach that minimizes user burden and maintains simplicity at the Bitcoin mainnet level. The proposed "Later" strategy is advantageous as it allows users to gradually opt into new security measures without overhauling their current practices immediately. This strategy ensures that the core experience remains straightforward and accessible, which is crucial for broad adoption and maintaining trust in the system.
Further, there is a suggestion to segregate more complex tasks such as identity verification, recovery processes, and advanced post-quantum workflows to optional, Bitcoin-aligned layers. This would likely include systems like Lightning and ProofnetBTC, which are capable of handling these sophisticated functions while still finalizing transactions on the main Bitcoin network. This separation aims to foster innovation and explore new possibilities without compromising the stability and simplicity of the Bitcoin mainnet, thus providing a balanced solution to future-proofing the cryptocurrency against quantum threats.
